summary: Project restarted with clean state. Accurate timing and logging begins now.

details.reason: Previous entries had fabricated timestamps. Wiped for integrity.

summary: Market research started
Market research complete. Live sources: analogue.co, play.date, anbernic.com.

details.keyFinding: Anbernic owns specs race. Analogue ($219) and Playdate ($199) prove design-first audience pays. Gap: numbered collectible with strong identity.
details.recommendation: Limited run collectible — compete on story, craft, rarity

summary: Product decision started
Product decided: pocket-sized collectible retro handheld, 10-20 numbered units, AI-designed.

details.shell: CNC aluminum or high-grade polycarbonate, matte
details.differentiator: AI-designed origin story, ships with design lineage card

summary: Design exploration started — sending prompt to Vizcom
4 design directions generated in Vizcom by DEV_DESIGNER_V1 agent.
Design refinement started — will pick best direction and iterate.
4 photorealistic renders generated and visually verified in browser.

details.verificationMethod: Each thumbnail URL opened and screenshotted individually
details.note: First attempt used wrong prompt producing cartoon sketches — redone with photorealistic prompt

summary: Design refinement started.
3 refined variations generated and verified. Selected: Retro Premium Brushed Metal.

details.reason: Brushed metal faceplate with burgundy buttons best fits collector aesthetic — premium materials, strong visual identity, clearly a design object not a toy

summary: Final design step started — generating hero render, side profile, and top-down view.
Final renders complete: hero shot, side profile, rear view — all visually verified.

details.note: First hero shot was off-brief (unicorn), re-run with explicit material/color description

summary: Engineering step started — generating BOM and spec sheet.
Engineering spec and BOM complete. Est. cost: $2,000-$4,200 for 20 units. Within budget.
Searching for manufacturers for CNC shells, PCB assembly, and full device assembly.
Vizcom extract-parts feature run on final design. 8 components identified with materials, finishes, and manufacturing processes.

details.method: Vizcom DEV_DESIGNER_V1 chat bar — extract parts + BOM prompt

summary: find_manufacturers complete. Vendors sourced for CNC shell (PCBWay), buttons (Fictiv), PCB+PCBA (JLCPCB), compute module (Radxa CM3/RK3566), display (Waveshare), battery (Aliexpress), laser engraving (SendCutSend). BOM export CSV generated from Vizcom GraphQL API. Total 20-unit estimate: $3,840–$4,224 incl. contingency.
human_approval step started. Spend proposal submitted. Awaiting Amy's approve/deny decision before manufacture begins.

5 vendor quote request drafts saved to amy.zhou@vizcom.com Gmail Drafts via IMAP. Recipients: JLCPCB (PCB), PCBWay (CNC shell), Waveshare (display), Radxa (CM3), Pimoroni (Pi Zero 2W). Amy to review and send.
